era_believed.statement: The Force is an energy field created by all living things that binds the galaxy together.
Lightsabers are elegant weapons constructed by Jedi Knights using kyber crystals as their power source.
The Death Star was a moon-sized space station capable of destroying entire planets with a single blast.
Darth Vader was once the Jedi Knight Anakin Skywalker before turning to the dark side of the Force.
C-3PO is a protocol droid fluent in over six million forms of communication built by Anakin Skywalker.
The Galactic Empire ruled through fear and military might under Emperor Palpatine's iron fist.
Hyperspace travel allows starships to cross the entire galaxy in hours through an alternate dimension.
The planet Alderaan was destroyed by the Death Star's superlaser on the orders of Grand Moff Tarkin.
Luke Skywalker destroyed the first Death Star by firing proton torpedoes into its thermal exhaust port.
Han Solo is a smuggler who pilots the Millennium Falcon, the fastest ship in the galaxy.
R2-D2 is an astromech droid that carried the stolen Death Star plans inside its memory banks.
The Jedi Order was an ancient organization of Force-sensitive peacekeepers that guarded the Galactic Republic.
Emperor Palpatine is a Sith Lord who secretly manipulated the Republic into becoming the Galactic Empire.
Chewbacca is a Wookiee warrior from the planet Kashyyyk who serves as co-pilot of the Millennium Falcon.
The Clone Wars were fought between the Galactic Republic's clone army and the Separatist droid forces.
Princess Leia Organa led the Rebel Alliance's fight against the Galactic Empire from hidden bases.
Tatooine is a desert planet with twin suns where both Anakin and Luke Skywalker grew up.
The Millennium Falcon made the Kessel Run in less than twelve parsecs by navigating a dangerous shortcut.
Yoda was a nine-hundred-year-old Jedi Grand Master who trained Luke Skywalker on the swamp planet Dagobah.
Midi-chlorians are microscopic life forms that reside in the cells of all living things and channel the Force.
The Sith follow the Rule of Two, maintaining only a master and an apprentice at any given time.
Coruscant is the capital planet of the galaxy, covered entirely by one massive city spanning its whole surface.
Han Solo was frozen in carbonite by Darth Vader and delivered to the crime lord Jabba the Hutt.
The second Death Star was destroyed during the Battle of Endor with the Emperor aboard.
Ewoks are small furry creatures native to the forest moon of Endor who helped defeat the Empire.
Obi-Wan Kenobi trained both Anakin Skywalker and his son Luke in the ways of the Force.
The Rebel Alliance operated from secret bases including one on the ice planet Hoth.
Boba Fett is a bounty hunter who wore Mandalorian armor and captured Han Solo for Jabba the Hutt.
Anakin Skywalker was born of no father and was believed to be the Chosen One of Jedi prophecy.
Podracers are high-speed repulsorlift vehicles used in dangerous races on Tatooine and other outer rim worlds.
Jabba the Hutt is a powerful crime lord who operates from a palace on the desert planet Tatooine.
Bacta tanks are medical devices filled with healing fluid that can mend severe injuries and wounds.
The Galactic Senate met on Coruscant to govern the Republic before Palpatine dissolved it.
Stormtroopers are the Empire's frontline soldiers clad in white plastoid armor deployed across the galaxy.
The Trade Federation blockaded the planet Naboo with a massive droid army at Palpatine's secret command.
Jedi can use the Force to move objects, influence minds, sense danger, and enhance their physical abilities.
Darth Maul was a Sith apprentice with a double-bladed lightsaber who killed Qui-Gon Jinn on Naboo.
The Wookiee home planet Kashyyyk is covered in enormous wroshyr trees that form multi-level forest canopies.
Holographic communication allows real-time visual messages to be transmitted across vast interstellar distances.
The Jedi Council met in a tower atop the Jedi Temple on Coruscant to guide the Order.
Count Dooku was a fallen Jedi who became Darth Tyranus and led the Separatist movement against the Republic.
Lando Calrissian administered Cloud City, a tibanna gas mining facility floating in the atmosphere of Bespin.
The Force has a light side and a dark side, with the Sith drawing power from anger and hatred.
Anakin Skywalker won the Boonta Eve Classic podrace on Tatooine as a young slave boy.
General Grievous was a cyborg military commander of the Separatist droid army who collected Jedi lightsabers.
The Clone Army was grown on the ocean planet Kamino from the genetic template of bounty hunter Jango Fett.
Blasters are the most common ranged weapons in the galaxy, firing bolts of concentrated energy plasma.
The Sith were an ancient order of dark side Force users dedicated to gaining power and destroying the Jedi.
Naboo is a lush green planet home to both the Naboo people and the aquatic Gungan species.
Mace Windu was a senior Jedi Master who wielded a distinctive purple-bladed lightsaber.
The Tusken Raiders are nomadic warriors native to Tatooine who are hostile to settlers and travelers.
Droids are sentient mechanical beings that serve various roles throughout galactic civilization.
The Sarlacc is a creature living in a pit on Tatooine that digests its victims over a thousand years.
Palpatine executed Order 66, which forced clone troopers to turn on and murder their Jedi commanders.
The moisture farms of Tatooine extract water from the atmosphere using vaporator technology.
Jedi younglings were trained at the Jedi Temple from early childhood in the ways of the Force.
Darth Vader's suit and helmet are a life support system keeping him alive after severe burn injuries.
The Rebel Alliance's symbol is a starbird that represents hope against the tyranny of the Empire.
Kyber crystals are Force-attuned gems found on planets like Ilum that power lightsaber blades.
The Cantina in Mos Eisley spaceport on Tatooine is a gathering place for smugglers and bounty hunters.
AT-AT walkers are massive four-legged armored transports used by the Empire for ground assault operations.
Anakin Skywalker built C-3PO from spare parts as a child to help his mother on Tatooine.
The Jedi used Force ghosts to communicate with the living after their physical death.
Qui-Gon Jinn was the Jedi Master who discovered Anakin Skywalker and believed he was the Chosen One.
TIE fighters are the Empire's standard starfighter, recognizable by their twin ion engines and screaming sound.
The planet Hoth is a frozen ice world where the Rebel Alliance hid its secret Echo Base.
X-wing starfighters are the Rebel Alliance's primary attack craft used in the assault on the Death Star.
Jabba's palace housed a rancor beast in a pit beneath his throne room used to execute prisoners.
The Force allows some Jedi to see visions of the future, though these visions are often unclear.
Darth Vader redeemed himself by killing Emperor Palpatine to save his son Luke Skywalker.
Ion cannons can disable the electronic systems of starships without destroying them.
The Jedi Archives on Coruscant contained records of virtually every star system in the known galaxy.
Tractor beams are projected energy fields that can capture and pull in starships against their will.
Droid restraining bolts are devices that limit a droid's free will and force compliance with commands.
The Battle of Yavin was the Rebel Alliance's first major victory against the Galactic Empire.
Wookiees can live for several hundred years and are known for their great strength and fierce loyalty.
Bespin is a gas giant planet where Cloud City floats high in the habitable zone of its atmosphere.
Speeder bikes are fast repulsorlift vehicles used for scouting and rapid transport across rough terrain.
The Imperial Star Destroyer is the Empire's primary capital ship, over a mile long and bristling with weapons.
Dagobah is a swamp planet strong with the Force where Yoda lived in exile for decades.
C-3PO and R2-D2 served together through the Clone Wars, the Galactic Civil War, and the fall of the Empire.
Bounty hunters are licensed mercenaries who track down and capture or kill targets for payment across the galaxy.
The Galactic Empire maintained control through a vast military including the Imperial Navy and Stormtrooper Corps.
Force-sensitive children were identified and recruited by the Jedi Order from across the galaxy for training.
Sand People of Tatooine ride single file on banthas to hide their numbers from potential threats.
The Rebel Alliance evolved into the New Republic after defeating the Empire at the Battle of Endor.
Astromech droids like R2-D2 serve as copilots and repair units on starfighters throughout the galaxy.
The spice mines of Kessel are a notorious source of valuable spice run by slave labor.
Mandalorian armor is made from beskar steel, one of the strongest and rarest metals in the galaxy.
Mon Mothma was a senator who became the civilian leader of the Rebel Alliance against the Empire.
The Jedi Mind Trick allows Force users to influence the thoughts and actions of weak-minded individuals.
Hyperdrives allow ships to enter hyperspace by generating a field that propels them faster than light.
The Battle of Geonosis was the first engagement of the Clone Wars between Republic clones and Separatist droids.
Jawas are small scavenging creatures on Tatooine who capture and sell droids from their massive sandcrawlers.
Admiral Ackbar commanded the Rebel fleet at the Battle of Endor and identified the Imperial trap.
Luke Skywalker trained a new generation of Jedi after the fall of the Galactic Empire.
The Gungan species live in underwater bubble cities on the planet Naboo beneath its vast lakes.
Darth Vader's iconic breathing apparatus produces a mechanical respiratory sound heard throughout the galaxy.
Yoda trained Jedi for over eight hundred years before going into exile on Dagobah.
The destruction of Alderaan by the Death Star killed billions of people in an instant.
Thermal detonators are powerful handheld explosives capable of destroying everything within a large blast radius.
The dark side of the Force offers quick power but corrupts its users physically and mentally.
A-wing interceptors are the fastest starfighters in the Rebel Alliance fleet, used for hit-and-run attacks.
The planet Mustafar is a volcanic world where Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi fought their final duel.
Palpatine used Anakin's fear of losing Padme to manipulate him into becoming the Sith Lord Darth Vader.
Proton torpedoes are guided projectiles carried by starfighters capable of penetrating heavy armor and shields.
The Jedi Code forbids attachment and romantic relationships, requiring emotional detachment from Jedi Knights.
Padme Amidala was the Queen of Naboo and later a senator who secretly married Anakin Skywalker.
Luke and Leia are the twin children of Anakin Skywalker and Padme Amidala separated at birth for protection.
The Battle of Endor saw the destruction of the second Death Star and the death of Emperor Palpatine.
Wampa ice creatures inhabit the frozen caves of Hoth and prey on tauntauns and unwary travelers.
The Jedi Temple on Coruscant served as the headquarters and training academy of the Jedi Order for millennia.
Star Destroyers carry wings of TIE fighters and can subjugate entire star systems through orbital bombardment.
Death sticks are an addictive narcotic substance sold in the lower levels of Coruscant's entertainment districts.
The carbonite freezing process preserves living beings in a state of perfect hibernation for indefinite transport.
Turbolasers are massive ship-mounted weapons that form the primary armament of capital ships in naval combat.
C-3PO was dismantled and carried as pieces on Chewbacca's back after being shot by stormtroopers on Cloud City.
Shield generators protect capital ships and planetary installations from enemy fire using deflector energy fields.
The Jedi Temple's five spires were visible across the Coruscant skyline and symbolized the Order's authority.
Repulsorlift technology allows speeders, airspeeders, and landspeeders to hover above the ground across all worlds.
